overview: This five-color deck leverages elemental synergy to control the board while generating consistent value. Wistfulness provides targeted tutoring to find key threats, while Vibrance offers card advantage and mana filtering. Ashling serves as the primary win condition by producing tokens and dealing direct damage, supported by Rekindled for recursion and late-game sustainability.
win_conditions: Direct damage from Ashling, Rekindled, and generated tokens
Card advantage and filtering from Vibrance enabling consistent value loops

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two lands and an early elemental or Wistfulness
Draws containing Vibrance paired with a relevant elemental target
Balanced color distribution supporting five-color mana base
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (preferably fetchable duals or lands that enter untapped), a 1-drop (Vibrance), and at least one early interaction or threat.
mulligan_criteria: Zero lands or more than four lands
No early plays or elemental synergies
Heavy concentration of late-game recursion without setup
Mulligan if you have 0-1 lands, only single-colored lands without fetch support, or a hand consisting entirely of high-cost spells with no early game plan.

early_game: Establish mana base and play low-cost elementals
Cast Wistfulness to tutor for immediate threats or answers
Avoid overcommitting resources before establishing board presence
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Vibrance if possible to establish early board presence.
Turn 2: Play a land, cast Wistfulness or a 2-drop threat, or use removal on an opposing early threat.
Sequence lands to ensure colored mana availability for 5-color spells, using fetch lands to find duals that tap for your next required color.
mid_game: Deploy Ashling to generate tokens and apply pressure
Use Vibrance to filter draws and find recursion or finishers
Maintain board control while cycling through the deck efficiently
Balance mana for 5-color spells while holding up interaction.
Use fetch lands strategically to avoid flooding and ensure consistent colored mana.
Protect key graveyard resources and use graveyard hate when necessary to disrupt opponent strategies.
Commit threats when you have removal or counterspells to protect them.
Hold back if the opponent has sweepers or heavy interaction, and prioritize efficient trades to maintain board advantage.
late_game: Leverage Rekindled to return key elementals from graveyard
Sustain token production to outvalue opponent's removal
Close out games with direct damage and overwhelming board state
Ashling tutoring for Rekindled, or overwhelming board presence with elementals.
Get there by stabilizing early, drawing into key pieces, and using removal to clear the path for your finisher.
When you have card advantage, protection for your finisher, or the opponent is low on interaction.
Also commit when you can out-value the opponent's remaining resources.

key_cards.name: Wistfulness
key_cards.role: Targeted tutor for elementals and key spells
key_cards.notes: Cast early to find immediate threats or answers; prioritize tutoring for Ashling or board control spells based on matchup

key_cards.name: Vibrance
key_cards.notes: Use to draw cards and filter unwanted lands or spells; hold up appropriate colors to maximize utility and maintain five-color consistency

key_cards.name: Ashling, Rekindled
key_cards.role: Primary win condition and token producer
key_cards.notes: Protect at all costs; use token generation to trade efficiently and apply direct damage; synergizes with recursion for repeated value

key_cards.name: Rekindled
key_cards.notes: Target high-impact elementals or Ashling from graveyard; use in late game to outlast opponent's resources and close games

sequencing_tips: Cast Wistfulness before committing to large spells to ensure optimal targets
Hold up mana for Vibrance's filter ability to maintain color consistency
Protect Ashling with blockers or counterspells to preserve token generation
Sequence Rekindled after opponent plays removal to maximize value
Common mistakes include overcommitting early, mismanaging fetch lands, failing to track opponent interaction, and not adapting to the metagame.
Unique interactions include Ashling tutoring for multiple win conditions, Vibrance synergizing with token strategies, and Wistfulness providing card advantage but being vulnerable to discard.
Always prioritize mana base consistency and interaction availability over raw power.

matchups.advice: Prioritize board presence and token generation to pressure life total before they stabilize; use Wistfulness to find answers to their key spells.
Use early elementals and Wistfulness to find removal or blockers; delay Ashling until you can protect it and generate value.
Race with token production while using Vibrance to filter for recursion; avoid trading efficiently into their value engines.
Disrupt their setup with early interaction; use Wistfulness to find specific answers and maintain board presence to delay their win condition.
Apply early pressure before they accelerate; use Rekindled and Ashling to outvalue their late-game threats once they commit resources.
Match their pace with efficient elementals and token generation; use Vibrance to filter for recursion and close games quickly.
Use cheap spells, split your combo, or use uncounterable threats. Hold up interaction to protect key plays and force the opponent to use counterspells on non-critical spells.
Win the resource war. Prioritize card draw, protect your finisher, and use targeted removal efficiently. Track the opponent's interaction and commit threats when they are out of answers.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells (hold up blue mana), sweepers (hold back board), and combo pieces (keep interaction ready).
Key tells: Opponent tapping out on turn 2/3 often signals a combo or control plan, while aggressive early plays indicate a need for immediate stabilization.
